title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for accessible puzzle interface and multichannel feedback and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Accessibility Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A quiz puzzle team handles case 9791 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; the prior good state must survive failure; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.

stimulus.title: Accessibility Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: WCAG-oriented code inspection alone proves the desktop puzzle is accessible.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Accessibility Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 8172 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; the prior good state must survive failure; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
